text:
Permanent BBE Restriction on 5-Year Seller Account with 99.6% Feedback – Trying to Understand.

Hi everyone, I’m posting here because I’m genuinely trying to understand what happened with my account and whether anyone has experienced something similar. I have been selling on eBay for about 5 years , and during that time I’ve built what I believe is a very strong record. My feedback score is 99.6% , with over 600 positive feedbacks overall . Over the last year alone , I received 287 positive feedback ratings, 4 neutral and only 2 negative . I also have a Purple Star and my account has always been Above Standard in every seller metric . Customer satisfaction has always been my priority. Whenever a buyer has an issue, I resolve it immediately and give the buyer several options depending on what they prefer: • replacement • full refund • partial refund while keeping the item • or return with full refund I never argue with buyers and I always try to resolve problems quickly and fairly. Over the past three months , I had 8 cases opened . All of them were resolved quickly, and some were even closed by the buyers themselves after we communicated and solved the issue. According to my CBT dashboard , I had: • 0% in the other two BBE categories • 3.6% in the SNAD category All the cases counted in that percentage were fully resolved with the buyers , and in many situations I even allowed buyers to keep the item while still providing a refund if that was their preference. Most of the issues were related to items arriving damaged . After investigating, I realized that this might have been related to a packaging change I had recently made. As soon as I noticed the spike in the metrics, I immediately switched back to my previous packaging method , which had worked reliably for years. My account was then placed under monitoring for about a month , but realistically the percentage cannot decrease significantly in such a short period of time. During that monitoring period two additional return cases were opened , but both were resolved quickly: • one turned out to be a buyer assembly misunderstanding , which the buyer later realized • the other involved a broken accessory , which I immediately replaced and the buyer closed the case himself Despite all of this, my account ended up being restricted under the BBE policy , with “No Selling Privilege / Permanently Restricted” now showing on my CBT dashboard. What I am struggling to understand is how an account with 5 years of history, 99.6% feedback, a Purple Star, and Above Standard metrics can suddenly be considered a risk to the marketplace , especially when every case was resolved and buyers were always prioritized. Even if someone were to manually review the full account history, I honestly don’t understand how the conclusion could be that my account poses a threat to buyers or to the platform. My account manager has already submitted an appeal for my case, which unfortunately was initially denied, but she has subm...
title: Permanent BBE Restriction on 5-Year Seller Account with 99.6% Feedback – Trying to Understand.

text:
Account Below Standard

On November 20th, I received a notice from ebay that my account is now considered below standard. The numbers were an average from a 12 month period which put my defect rate around 2.7%. Almost all of the defects were having to cancel an item because I couldn't find it. Looking back, I should have put my store in vacation mode a few times while I worked on organizing my inventory and the 3 platforms they are on. I work a full-time job in addition to selling on ebay, etc. but I'll be retiring in June and will have a lot more time to get things in order. In the meantime, I've tried everything I can think of to get out of below-standard status. I've had a 40% off clearance sale, offered a discount coupon every month, improved listings, photos, and most importantly, I haven't had any more defects since November. Unfortunately, though, I'm still below standard, and I'm not sure how long it will take to improve. I'm not allowed to promote my items as a below-standard seller, so my items are getting no boost in visibility at all. My sales have plummeted since November, so it's harder to improve my defect percentage rate. It's safe to say I've learned my lesson in a major way and want to get back on track with ebay but what I've done so far hasn't worked. Any ideas?
title: Account Below Standard

text:
Sellers using fraudulent tracking numbers

Back in november I made a purchase on ebay and within the next day or two the item was marked as shipped but no tracking was provided. The day it was supposed to be delivered I messaged the seller and asked where the package was and why there was no tracking provided. They then changed the shipping details to show a tracking number that stated the item was delivered! I immediately opened a case with ebay and within a few weeks had my money refunded because nothing was ever delivered. Now I'm going through it all over again. This time instead of waiting I messaged the seller [removed by moderator] and asked why there was no tracking number provided. They "apologized" and said they will update the shipping details to show a tracking number and also sent me a picture of a USPS receipt with the same tracking number. I said ok thats fine and then entered the number on the USPS tracking website. What came up was exactly this: 'Tracking is not available for this item. This may be because the tracking number is invalid; USPS has not yet received payment; USPS has not yet received the item; the item does not include tracking; or other reasons.' The buyer had a receipt which meant USPS has the item therefore tracking would've stated that. So they somehow managed to create a picture (ai?) of the receipt which was cropped to only show the section of the receipt that states the destination, price, and tracking, not which USPS location or any other information. If this is the new norm on ebay then count me out. I can't believe ebay hasnt caught on to these scammers. It seems like theres dozens of "new sellers" with low feedback ratings scamming people, yet I feel like im the only one going through it since like I said it seems like ebay hasnt caught on. How can I contact ebay before the so called delivery date and let them know that there is a scam already in process?? Why do I have to wait weeks until I have my money refunded? And why isnt ebay doing anything to prevent these scammers from creating accounts??
title: Sellers using fraudulent tracking numbers

https://chainstoreage.com/ebay-klarna-expand-embedded-resale-six-new-markets "eBay and Klarna are bringing a direct integration between their platforms to six countries across three continents. In December 2024, eBay and Klarna embedded the online giant’s resale capabilities directly into the digital bank and flexible payments provider ’s app in the U.S. and U.K. This integration enables eligible purchases made with Klarna to be listed on eBay in a few taps. Product images, descriptions, and key details are automatically pre-filled using Klarna’s purchase data, eliminating manual listing creation. Based on strong engagement that has created more than 1 million eBay listings directly through the Klarna app, the companies are now expanding Klarna’s embedded resell integration with eBay to six new markets: Australia, Belgium, Canada, Ireland, Poland and Switzerland." There's more, but this seems to be the main point. IF---big IF---I'm understanding this correctly, it is saying that if I use Klarna (a payment provider, like PayPal I believe) to buy something (not just on ebay), and I later decide to resell what I bought, I can use the Klarna app to post a new listing to ebay with just a few clicks. Is that right? And if so, has anyone here actually used this?
title: Not exactly sure what this mean, but here you go....
